articles.title: Inflation target held at 4% for next five years

The framework, set for another five years until March 2031, obligates the RBI to manage monetary policy for price control. This move influences government fiscal measures, aiming for overall economic stability.

articles.description: A new bill aims to speed up bankruptcy resolution in India. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman introduced amendments to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code. The proposed changes focus on creditor-led, out-of-court settlements. This aims to reduce delays and improve the process for resolving insolvent companies. The bill also addresses cross-border and corporate group insolvency cases.

articles.title: Lok Sabha approves Finance Bill, tax rules eased

dailyNews.title: BBC taps ex-Google exec as new director general following Trump documentary controversy
dailyNews.description: The BBC named ex-Google chief Matt Brittin as its new director general following the controversy of its Trump documentary that led to multiple resignations.

dailyNews.title: White House unleashes on Raskin for clinging to 'deranged' Jack Smith 'lies' in latest Trump docs flare-up
dailyNews.description: The White House and DOJ are pushing back against Rep. Jamie Raskin's letter alleging Trump mishandled classified documents, calling the accusations baseless and a political stunt.

dailyNews.title: Trump scores 'massive court victory' with appellate ruling on ICE detention
dailyNews.description: The Eighth Circuit ruled illegal immigrants detained by ICE are not entitled to bond hearings, a win AG Bondi called a massive court victory.

dailyNews.title: Kate Marvel, Prominent Climate Scientist, Resigns From NASA
dailyNews.description: Kate Marvel, a well-known author, joins an estimated 95,000 people who have left federal science agencies since President Trump returned to the White House.

dailyNews.title: Matt Brittin, Former Google Executive, Named the New BBC Head
dailyNews.description: Matt Brittin, the former president of Google in Europe, will become the new director general. Among the items on his to-do list: handling a lawsuit from President Trump.

dailyNews.title: Trump Cannot See That the Opposition Is Real
dailyNews.description: What happens when a narcissist goes to war.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: Additional Troops Deployed to Middle East as Trump Claims Victory
dailyNews.description: More than 1,000 additional troops from the 82nd Airborne Division are heading to the Middle East, according to two sources. The announcement came as President Donald Trump in the Oval Office Tuesday said, “We’ve won this, this war has been won.” NBC's Richard Engel reports for TODAY on the war in Iran.
